prompt: Do not use <think> tags.
Do not reason before generating.
Your response must begin with <nn> immediately.
Your first output character must be '<'.
Do not write any text before <nn>.
Do not write any text after </tr>.
Output only these three XML blocks in order: <nn>...</nn><hp>...</hp><tr>...</tr>.
Your task is to generate a LEMUR neural network that improves '{metric}' beyond {acc_2} at epoch {epoch} on '{dataset}' for '{task}'.
References A and B are structurally similar — study them and produce an improved variant.
The <hp> block must contain a valid JSON dict with exactly these keys: batch, dropout, epoch, lr, momentum, transform.
The 'transform' value in <hp> must be exactly 'norm_256_flip'.
supported_hyperparameters() must return exactly: 'batch', 'dropout', 'epoch', 'lr', 'momentum', 'transform'.
in_shape is a 4-tuple (batch, channels, height, width) — never unpack it as (C, H, W); read channels as in_shape[1].
supported_hyperparameters() must be defined as a top-level function OUTSIDE all classes — the file is invalid without it.
Read prm['epoch'] inside train_setup or learn — every hyperparameter key must actually be used in the code.
Reference A (accuracy={acc_1}):
<hp>{hp_1}</hp>
<tr>{tr_1}</tr>
<nn>{nn_1}</nn>
Reference B (accuracy={acc_2}):
<hp>{hp_2}</hp>
<tr>{tr_2}</tr>
<nn>{nn_2}</nn>
